title: Who won the Perez vs Lalami Laaroussi ATP Challenger tennis match on May 5, 2026?
rules: If Brandon Perez wins the Perez vs Lalami Laaroussi professional tennis match in the 2026 ATP Challenger Brazzaville Round Of 32 after a ball has been played, then the market resolves to Yes.
tavily_brief:
[redacted] Lalami Laaroussi achieved a career-high ranking of 905 on April 13, 2026, while Perez's peak was 970 in August 2025. The ranking gap of roughly 500 positions represents a substantial quality differential. **Recent Performance:** Perez entered the tournament in poor form, posting a 0-6 record in 2026 through late April. He lost consecutively in qualifying rounds at Abidjan 2 (April 27, 6-1, 5-7, 6-2 to Adil Kalyanpur), Abidjan 1 (April 20), and earlier tournaments in February and January. His 2026 win-loss record stood at 0-7 before Brazzaville. Lalami Laaroussi showed mixed recent results: he [redacted]

**Surface & Context:** The tournament was played on clay courts in Brazzaville. Lalami Laaroussi's career record shows 59-53 on clay (52.7% win rate) versus Perez's 34-42 on clay (44.7% win rate), favoring Lalami Laaroussi on this surface. Perez entered as an alternate, suggesting lower seeding status. The match was scheduled for May 5 at the Round of 32 stage. **Head-to-Head:** No prior ATP-level matches existed between these players, eliminating direct historical precedent. **Key Factors:** Lalami Laaroussi's superior ranking, better recent form despite losses, clay-court experience advantage, and Perez's extended losing streak all favor the Moroccan player. However, Perez's entry as an alternate and his desperate need for a win could provide motivation. **Most Important Facts:** (1) Lalami Laaroussi held a ~500-ranking-point advantage (869 vs. 1,364) entering the match. (2) Perez was winless in 2026 (0-6 through April 27), while Lalami Laaroussi had won recent matches despite recent losses. (3) Lalami Laaroussi showed superior clay-court credentials with a 52.7% career clay win rate versus Perez's 44.7%.
